metadata.title: RSV Evidence Map: immunization, protection, and correlates
metadata.surveillance_cadence: Weekly automated surveillance using GitHub Actions across PubMed, medRxiv, ClinicalTrials.gov, Crossref, and selected official guidance/regulatory pages.
metadata.status_note: Records may be automatically ingested and algorithmically classified; auto-added records are not manually verified.
metadata.public_summary: Rapid RSV evidence map: records are coded into a matrix by RSV evidence domain and evidence type. The seed map is source-backed; newly detected PubMed, medRxiv, ClinicalTrials.gov, CDC/ACIP, FDA, WHO, EMA, PHAC/NACI and UKHSA/JCVI records may be added automatically and labelled as not manually verified.

records.citation: Coindy et al., 2024 review
records.title: Antibody-mediated protection against RSV disease in children
records.product_platform: Maternal antibodies; monoclonal antibodies; vaccine-induced antibodies
records.key_finding: Reviews evidence that antibodies have a central role in protection against severe RSV in children.
records.evidence_signal: Mechanistic evidence synthesis
records.actionability: Frames antibody-based prevention and correlate-development questions.
records.tags: antibodies; children; correlate; review
records.evidence_update_basis: publication year only in rapid coding; conservative 1 January placeholder

records.citation: Ruckwardt et al., Lancet Respiratory Medicine 2021
records.title: Safety, tolerability, and immunogenicity of the respiratory syncytial virus prefusion F vaccine DS-Cav1
records.key_finding: Evaluated safety and immune responses to DS-Cav1, reinforcing the feasibility of prefusion F vaccine approaches.
records.evidence_signal: Early human RCT evidence
records.actionability: Useful bridge evidence for antigen design and immunogenicity, not direct clinical efficacy.
records.tags: DS-Cav1; phase 1; immunogenicity; safety
records.evidence_update_basis: source publication month

records.citation: Crank et al., Science 2019
records.title: A proof of concept for structure-based vaccine design targeting RSV in humans
records.key_finding: Provided clinical proof-of-concept that structure-based prefusion F design can elicit strong RSV-neutralising responses in humans.
records.evidence_signal: Early human immunogenicity evidence
records.actionability: Connects antigen-design evidence to vaccine candidate development.
records.tags: DS-Cav1; phase 1; immunogenicity; structure-based vaccine
records.evidence_update_basis: source publication date

records.citation: Frivold et al., Nature Communications 2025
records.title: RSV-specific antibodies and risk of symptomatic or lower respiratory RSV disease
records.population: Infants; children; adults depending on cohort
records.outcome_domain: Symptomatic disease; lower respiratory disease; correlate uncertainty
records.key_finding: Supports antibody associations with protection while illustrating that consensus protective thresholds remain incompletely defined across populations.
records.evidence_signal: Emerging correlate evidence
records.actionability: Identifies need for standardised assays and population-specific thresholds.
records.tags: correlate; antibodies; threshold; assay standardisation
records.evidence_update_basis: source publication date

records.citation: Fong et al., Open Forum Infectious Diseases 2023
records.title: Antibody correlates of protection from severe RSV disease in a vaccine efficacy trial
records.study_design: Correlates analysis nested in maternal vaccine efficacy trial
records.product_platform: RSV F nanoparticle maternal vaccine; antibody measurements
records.outcome_domain: Correlates of risk/protection; severe hypoxia endpoint
records.key_finding: Found evidence supporting RSV-neutralising and F-binding antibody measurements as inverse correlates of severe infant RSV disease risk in trial data.
records.evidence_signal: Correlate evidence from clinical trial cohort
records.actionability: Promising but requires validation across products, populations, and endpoints.
records.tags: correlate; maternal immunisation; antibodies; infants
records.evidence_update_basis: source publication date

records.citation: Ngwuta et al., Science Translational Medicine 2015
records.title: Prefusion F-specific antibodies determine the magnitude of RSV neutralizing activity in human sera
records.key_finding: Found RSV neutralising activity in human sera was primarily derived from pre-F-specific antibodies.
records.evidence_signal: Mechanistic correlate evidence
records.actionability: Supports neutralising antibody endpoints as an evidence bridge, while not alone proving clinical protection thresholds.
records.tags: prefusion F; neutralising antibodies; correlate; human sera
records.evidence_update_basis: source publication date

records.citation: Krarup et al., Nature Communications 2015
records.title: A highly stable prefusion RSV F vaccine derived from structural analysis of the fusion mechanism
records.key_finding: A highly stable prefusion F immunogen elicited neutralising antibodies and protected cotton rats in challenge experiments.
records.evidence_signal: Preclinical proof-of-concept
records.actionability: Supports preclinical-to-clinical vaccine design rationale.
records.tags: prefusion F; animal model; vaccine antigen; protection
records.evidence_update_basis: source publication date

records.citation: McLellan et al., Science 2013
records.title: Structure of RSV fusion glycoprotein trimer bound to a prefusion-specific neutralizing antibody
records.study_design: Structural biology / human sera neutralisation analysis
records.population: Human sera; RSV F antigen design
records.key_finding: Showed that the prefusion state of RSV F is the target of most RSV-neutralising activity in human sera.
records.evidence_signal: Foundational mechanistic evidence
records.actionability: Core rationale for prefusion F vaccines and antibody products.
records.tags: prefusion F; neutralising antibody; structural biology; correlate
records.evidence_update_basis: source publication date

records.citation: McLellan et al., Science 2013
records.title: Structure-based design of a fusion glycoprotein vaccine for RSV
records.population: Preclinical models; RSV F antigen design
records.key_finding: Designed stabilised prefusion F immunogens that preserved neutralisation-sensitive antigenic sites.
records.evidence_signal: Foundational translational evidence
records.actionability: Established design logic for modern RSV pre-F vaccines.
records.tags: DS-Cav1; prefusion F; antigen design; vaccine platform
records.evidence_update_basis: source publication date

records.citation: CDC Adult RSV vaccine guidance
records.title: RSV Vaccine Guidance for Adults
records.population: Adults aged 75+; adults 50–74 at increased risk; adults 18–49 pending CDC recommendation contexts
records.key_finding: Recommends a single RSV vaccine dose for all adults 75+ and adults 50–74 at increased risk; no product preference and no annual revaccination recommendation at present.
records.evidence_signal: Authoritative policy guidance
records.actionability: Current adult implementation anchor.
records.tags: adult guidance; CDC; eligibility; revaccination; product choice
records.evidence_update_basis: CDC page date

records.citation: Britton et al., MMWR 2024
records.title: Use of RSV Vaccines in Adults Aged 60 Years and Older: Updated ACIP Recommendations
records.population: Adults aged 60 years and older; high-risk groups
records.outcome_domain: Vaccine uptake; safety; effectiveness; policy refinement
records.key_finding: Replaced shared clinical decision-making with age- and risk-based recommendations after first-season uptake, safety and effectiveness data became available.
records.evidence_signal: Policy synthesis
records.actionability: Useful for mapping how evidence translated into updated recommendations.
records.tags: adult guidance; ACIP; policy; uptake; safety
records.evidence_update_basis: MMWR issue date

records.citation: Ison et al., Lancet Respiratory Medicine 2025
records.title: RSVPreF3 OA efficacy, safety, and immunogenicity over three RSV seasons
records.key_finding: Single-dose RSVPreF3 OA remained efficacious over three seasons, with evidence of waning and unresolved optimal revaccination strategy.
records.evidence_signal: Longer-term phase 3 evidence
records.actionability: Key record for duration-of-protection and booster-policy gaps.
records.tags: adult vaccine; durability; waning; Arexvy; three seasons
records.evidence_update_basis: source publication date

records.citation: Ison et al., Clinical Infectious Diseases 2024
records.title: Efficacy and Safety of RSVPreF3 OA over two RSV seasons
records.outcome_domain: Durability; revaccination; lower respiratory tract disease
records.key_finding: One dose remained efficacious over two RSV seasons; revaccination after one year was evaluated for safety and immunogenicity.
records.evidence_signal: Durability evidence from phase 3 programme
records.actionability: Informs revaccination-interval uncertainty.
records.tags: adult vaccine; durability; revaccination; Arexvy
records.evidence_update_basis: source publication date

records.citation: Papi et al., NEJM 2023
records.title: Respiratory Syncytial Virus Prefusion F Protein Vaccine in Older Adults
records.outcome_domain: RSV-related lower respiratory tract disease; severe disease; safety
records.key_finding: Single-dose RSVPreF3 OA showed high efficacy against RSV-related lower respiratory tract disease in older adults with acceptable safety in the pivotal trial.
records.evidence_signal: Pivotal phase 3 efficacy evidence
records.actionability: Supports adult vaccine recommendations and product licensure.
records.tags: adult vaccine; older adults; Arexvy; RSVPreF3; phase 3
records.evidence_update_basis: source publication date

records.citation: Walsh et al., NEJM 2023
records.title: Efficacy and Safety of a Bivalent RSV Prefusion F Vaccine in Older Adults
records.outcome_domain: RSV-associated lower respiratory tract illness; acute respiratory illness; safety
records.key_finding: RSVpreF prevented RSV-associated lower respiratory tract illness and acute respiratory illness in older adults without evident safety concerns in the pivotal trial.
records.evidence_signal: Pivotal phase 3 efficacy evidence
records.actionability: Supports adult vaccine recommendations and licensure.
records.tags: adult vaccine; older adults; Abrysvo; RSVpreF; phase 3
records.evidence_update_basis: source publication date

records.citation: Wilson et al., NEJM 2023
records.title: Efficacy and Safety of an mRNA-Based RSV PreF Vaccine in Older Adults
records.outcome_domain: RSV-associated lower respiratory tract disease; acute respiratory disease; safety
records.key_finding: A single mRNA-1345 dose reduced RSV-associated lower respiratory tract disease and acute respiratory disease in adults 60+ with no evident safety concerns in the trial report.
records.evidence_signal: Pivotal clinical efficacy evidence
records.actionability: Supports mRNA adult RSV vaccine use and platform comparison.
records.tags: adult vaccine; mRNA; mResvia; Moderna; phase 3
records.evidence_update_basis: source publication date

records.citation: Fry et al., JAMA Network Open 2025
records.title: Respiratory Syncytial Virus Vaccine for US Adults Aged 60 Years or Older
records.study_design: Large case-control study using electronic health data
records.population: US adults aged 60 years and older; immunocompromised subgroup
records.outcome_domain: Medically attended RSV; ED/urgent care; hospitalisation; safety signal
records.key_finding: Estimated vaccine effectiveness was approximately 75% against medically attended RSV outcomes, with a quantified Guillain-Barré syndrome safety signal requiring monitoring.
records.evidence_signal: Large real-world effectiveness and safety study
records.actionability: Supports benefits while highlighting rare-safety-signal surveillance needs.
records.tags: adult vaccine; real-world; GBS; safety; effectiveness
records.evidence_update_basis: source publication date

records.citation: Surie et al., JAMA 2024
records.title: RSV Vaccine Effectiveness Against Hospitalization Among US Adults Aged 60 Years and Older
records.population: US adults aged 60 years and older
records.product_platform: Licensed adult RSV vaccines in first season of use
records.key_finding: Post-licensure RSV vaccination was associated with reduced odds of RSV-associated hospitalisation during the first season of use.
records.evidence_signal: Real-world effectiveness evidence
records.actionability: Important implementation evidence beyond RCT efficacy endpoints.
records.tags: adult vaccine; real-world effectiveness; hospitalisation; United States
records.evidence_update_basis: source publication date

records.citation: CDC pregnant women guidance
records.title: RSV Vaccine Guidance for Pregnant Women
records.population: Pregnant women at 32 through 36 weeks gestation; infants
records.outcome_domain: Timing; eligibility; maternal vaccine vs infant antibody choice
records.key_finding: Recommends a single Pfizer Abrysvo dose during weeks 32–36 of pregnancy, with most infants not needing both maternal vaccine and infant monoclonal antibody.
records.evidence_signal: Authoritative policy guidance
records.actionability: Current implementation anchor for maternal immunisation.
records.tags: maternal guidance; CDC; Abrysvo; 32-36 weeks; implementation
records.evidence_update_basis: CDC page date

records.citation: Fleming-Dutra et al., MMWR 2023
records.title: Use of the Pfizer RSV Vaccine During Pregnancy for Prevention of RSV-Associated LRTI in Infants
records.outcome_domain: Policy; gestational timing; efficacy; safety warnings
records.key_finding: Recommended RSVpreF as a one-time dose at 32–36 weeks gestation, with FDA labelling to reduce potential preterm birth risk at earlier gestations.
records.evidence_signal: Policy synthesis with safety caveats
records.actionability: Provides rationale for gestational-window restrictions.
records.tags: maternal guidance; ACIP; gestational window; preterm birth warning
records.evidence_update_basis: MMWR issue date

records.citation: Kampmann et al., NEJM 2023
records.title: Bivalent Prefusion F Vaccine in Pregnancy to Prevent RSV Illness in Infants
records.study_design: Phase 3 randomised, placebo-controlled maternal immunisation trial (MATISSE)
records.population: Pregnant women; infants from birth to 6 months
records.outcome_domain: Medically attended severe RSV-LRTI; infant protection; safety
records.key_finding: Maternal RSVpreF vaccination was effective against medically attended severe RSV-associated lower respiratory tract illness in infants.
records.evidence_signal: Pivotal phase 3 maternal efficacy evidence
records.actionability: Supports maternal vaccination as a prevention pathway for early infant RSV disease.
records.tags: maternal vaccine; Abrysvo; infants; severe LRTI; phase 3
records.evidence_update_basis: source publication date

records.citation: Madhi et al., NEJM 2020
records.title: Respiratory Syncytial Virus Vaccination during Pregnancy and Effects in Infants
records.outcome_domain: Medically significant RSV-LRTI; infant endpoints; maternal safety
records.key_finding: The vaccine did not meet the prespecified success criterion for efficacy against RSV-associated medically significant lower respiratory tract infection in infants.
records.evidence_signal: Phase 3 negative/partial efficacy evidence
records.actionability: Important comparator showing maternal vaccine efficacy is product- and endpoint-dependent.
records.tags: maternal vaccine; Novavax; PREPARE; negative trial; infants
records.evidence_update_basis: source publication date

records.citation: Son et al., JAMA Network Open 2024
records.title: Nonadjuvanted Bivalent RSV Prefusion F Protein Subunit Vaccination During Pregnancy and Preterm Birth
records.population: Pregnant people delivering during 2023–2024 recommended vaccination period
records.outcome_domain: Preterm birth; real-world safety; hypertensive disorders
records.key_finding: A real-world cohort did not find an increased risk of preterm birth based on maternal RSVpreF vaccination status.
records.evidence_signal: Early post-licensure safety evidence
records.actionability: Adds reassurance but requires broader surveillance across settings.
records.tags: maternal vaccine; real-world safety; preterm birth; Abrysvo
records.evidence_update_basis: source publication date

records.citation: Dieussaert et al., NEJM 2024
records.title: RSV Prefusion F Protein-Based Maternal Vaccine
records.study_design: Phase 3 randomised maternal vaccine trial stopped early
records.outcome_domain: Infant efficacy; preterm birth; neonatal safety
records.key_finding: Enrollment and vaccination were stopped after a higher preterm birth risk was observed in the vaccine group than placebo.
records.evidence_signal: High-priority safety signal in phase 3 trial
records.actionability: Central safety record for maternal active vaccine evidence mapping.
records.tags: maternal vaccine; GSK; preterm birth; safety signal; stopped trial
records.evidence_update_basis: source publication date

records.citation: Moulia et al., MMWR 2025 clesrovimab recommendations
records.title: Use of Clesrovimab for Prevention of Severe RSV-Associated LRTI in Infants
records.study_design: ACIP recommendation / GRADE and evidence-to-recommendations synthesis
records.population: Infants younger than 8 months not otherwise protected by maternal vaccination
records.key_finding: Recommended clesrovimab as an alternative to nirsevimab for infants under 8 months entering or born during their first RSV season, with no preference between products.
records.evidence_signal: Policy synthesis / newest infant mAb recommendation
records.actionability: Updates product landscape and supply-resilience options.
records.tags: clesrovimab; nirsevimab; ACIP; infants; guidance
records.evidence_update_basis: MMWR issue date

records.citation: CDC infant and young child RSV guidance
records.title: RSV Immunization Guidance for Infants and Young Children
records.population: Infants under 8 months; selected children 8–19 months; not generally ≥20 months
records.product_platform: Nirsevimab; clesrovimab; maternal RSV vaccine linkage
records.outcome_domain: Eligibility; timing; product selection; second-season restrictions
records.key_finding: Explains which infants and young children should receive an infant RSV antibody and clarifies age/season restrictions.
records.evidence_signal: Authoritative clinical guidance
records.actionability: Practical implementation rule set for infant mAb programmes.
records.tags: infant guidance; monoclonal antibody; CDC; eligibility; timing
records.evidence_update_basis: CDC page date

records.citation: Jones et al., MMWR 2023
records.title: Use of Nirsevimab for the Prevention of RSV Disease Among Infants and Young Children
records.study_design: ACIP recommendation / GRADE and evidence-to-recommendations synthesis
records.population: Infants under 8 months; high-risk children 8–19 months entering second season
records.key_finding: Recommended nirsevimab for infants entering their first RSV season and selected high-risk children entering their second season.
records.evidence_signal: Policy synthesis with GRADE/EtR
records.actionability: Implementation anchor for nirsevimab use.
records.tags: nirsevimab; ACIP; infants; second season; guidance
records.evidence_update_basis: MMWR issue date

records.citation: Zar et al., NEJM 2025
records.title: Clesrovimab for Prevention of RSV Disease in Healthy Preterm and Full-Term Infants
records.population: Healthy preterm and full-term infants entering first RSV season
records.key_finding: A single fixed dose of clesrovimab reduced RSV-associated medically attended lower respiratory infection and hospitalisation with placebo-like safety profile in the trial report.
records.evidence_signal: Pivotal infant mAb efficacy evidence
records.actionability: Supports newer infant mAb option and comparison with nirsevimab strategies.
records.tags: clesrovimab; infants; monoclonal antibody; phase 2b/3; hospitalisation
records.evidence_update_basis: source updated date; original publication 2025-09-17

records.citation: Zar et al., NEJM 2025 letter / SMART
records.title: Clesrovimab in Infants and Children at Increased Risk for Severe RSV
records.study_design: Phase 3 open-label comparative trial / interim report
records.population: Infants and children at increased risk for severe RSV; first and second seasons
records.outcome_domain: Safety; high-risk prophylaxis; RSV medically attended LRTI/hospitalisation
records.key_finding: Assessed clesrovimab against palivizumab in high-risk infants and children, extending evidence beyond healthy first-season infants.
records.evidence_signal: Comparative high-risk infant evidence
records.actionability: Important for high-risk second-season policy and product substitution questions.
records.tags: clesrovimab; palivizumab; high-risk infants; SMART; comparative
records.evidence_update_basis: source publication date

records.citation: Drysdale et al., NEJM 2023
records.title: Nirsevimab for Prevention of Hospitalizations Due to RSV in Infants
records.population: Infants under 12 months entering RSV season
records.key_finding: Nirsevimab reduced hospitalisations for RSV-associated lower respiratory tract infection and very severe RSV-LRTI.
records.evidence_signal: Pragmatic RCT evidence
records.actionability: High-priority implementation evidence for preventing hospitalisation.
records.tags: nirsevimab; HARMONIE; infants; hospitalisation; pragmatic trial
records.evidence_update_basis: source publication date

records.citation: Hammitt et al., NEJM 2022
records.title: Nirsevimab for Prevention of RSV in Healthy Late-Preterm and Term Infants
records.population: Healthy late-preterm and term infants entering first RSV season
records.key_finding: A single nirsevimab injection before RSV season protected healthy late-preterm and term infants against medically attended RSV-associated LRTI.
records.evidence_signal: Pivotal infant mAb efficacy evidence
records.actionability: Supports universal first-season infant monoclonal antibody strategy.
records.tags: nirsevimab; infants; monoclonal antibody; phase 3; MELODY

records.citation: IMpact-RSV Study Group, Pediatrics 1998
records.title: Palivizumab, a humanized RSV monoclonal antibody, reduces RSV hospitalisation
records.population: Preterm infants; children with bronchopulmonary dysplasia / chronic lung disease
records.key_finding: Palivizumab prophylaxis reduced RSV-related hospitalisation by 55% in high-risk infants and young children.
records.evidence_signal: Foundational passive immunisation RCT

records.title: Nirsevimab Effectiveness Against ICU Admission and Acute Respiratory Failure in Infants
records.outcome_domain: ICU admission; acute respiratory failure; severe RSV outcomes
